pattern: Since only the multiset of digits is fixed, the smallest number from a set of digits is formed by sorting them ascending, so dealing every digit alternately into two piles after sorting balances both count and magnitude.
hints: The two numbers you build are entirely up to you — only the multiset of digits is fixed, and how you arrange them inside each number decides how large it becomes.

For any fixed set of digits, the smallest number they can form is reached by placing them in ascending order left to right; splitting the digits as evenly as possible in count also keeps both numbers short.

Sort every digit of the input once, then hand them out alternately into two growing digit lists — reading each list left to right in that same order already gives its minimal arrangement, so just sum the two resulting numbers.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct digits, minimum digit count
a zero digit lands in the more significant slot of one half
maximum value: nine zeros and a single one
largest nine-digit value, every digit the same
repeated zero digits force a leading zero in both halves
all digits identical, so the split is purely about balancing length
mixed digits including an interior zero

stress.description: random numbers of varying digit length without leading zeros
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = rng.randint(2, min(9, max(2, size)))
    first = rng.randint(1, 9)
    rest = [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(length - 1)]
    num = int(str(first) + ''.join(str(d) for d in rest))
    return [num]
